mechanism_narrative: A high level of the civilian unemployment rate signals that the economy is near a cyclical trough. Historically, this has been followed by policy stimulus and recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as bearish sentiment reverses.
failure_condition: If the correlation between UNRATE level and 5-day forward returns is negative over rolling 2-year windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops to or below 0.25%, the central bank is constrained at the zero lower bound and is likely to deploy unconventional easing measures. This expectation of further accommodation generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after hitting the zero lower bound, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the signal fails in subsequent ZLB episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of real retail sales (RSAFS) to industrial production (INDPRO) measures demand relative to supply. A cyclical low in this ratio signals excess inventory, which historically precedes a restocking cycle and rising equity prices over a 60-day horizon as the supply chain normalizes.
failure_condition: If the RSAFS/INDPRO ratio at its first percentile does not predict positive 60-day returns in out-of-sample recessions (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI YoY below 3%), a rise in the Consumer Price Index indicates strengthening aggregate demand without immediate monetary tightening. This leads to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: low inflation regime with CPI YoY below 3%
failure_condition: If during low-inflation periods, rising CPI is followed by negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month consecutive decline in industrial production signals a severe contraction. Historically, such streaks have been followed by a mean reversion and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as markets anticipate a recovery.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month negative for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If after a 3-month decline streak, 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances across historical recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarterly GDP growth rate indicates the economy is in recession. Markets often anticipate a policy response and subsequent recovery, leading to positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon after each negative GDP print.
failure_condition: If following negative GDP growth, 60-day forward returns are positive less than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of industrial production (INDPRO) to the unemployment rate (UNRATE) captures output per unemployed worker. When this ratio is at a cyclical low, it indicates inefficient labor utilization, and an eventual productivity rebound drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the INDPRO/UNRATE ratio at its fifth percentile does not predict positive 20-day returns in subsequent high-unemployment episodes (out-of-sample Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound regime (FEDFUNDS < 1%), a decline in industrial production signals that the economy remains in deep recession and prompts expectations of additional monetary and fiscal accommodation. This anticipated stimulus produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS below 1%, a one-standard-deviation decline in INDPRO does not lead to positive 5-day returns in at least 60% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.
